messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: @Kepler - YQLMesh in the meshcore app, there is a line of sight tool. try plotting between you and your friend’s location to see if there is anything in between. If there is no blockage, try to go as high as you can, at the top floor of your home by a window, or just stick the radio up by hand. before you have major repeaters installed at high elevation locations that can cover a large area, having repeaters on roof top at homes helps a lot.

messages.author: Altitude YYC
messages.text: I was messing around with MeshCore hashtag channels. I learned something new. As hashtag channels are like public channels; once you join the channel you are always "following" the channel. So you will receive new message notification as long as your node is turned on and connected to your mesh. If you leave the local mesh or turn off your node, you will not see any of the messages that were sent to the channel while you were offline. On the other hand, 'Room' servers will store messages (up to 32) and once you sign into the 'ROOM' and leave you node turned on you will receive notification of new messages. If you turn off your node or leave the local mesh you will have to log back into the 'Room' again to receive new messages. Seems like a hashtag channel linked with a Room Server would be a good combination for group or community chat. (edited)

messages.text: Your region is now onboarded into MeshMapper: https://yql.meshmapper.net/ It will take a few hours to populate all the repeaters, as they will appear as they advert themselves. To use the soon-to-be-deprecated WebApp (once the apps are officially released on the app stores): Android 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App. 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore App. 3. Open Google Chrome and go to: https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ 4. Connect your device to the page. 5. Choose your scan interval and whether you're using an external antenna or not. 6. Send a ping, or enable Auto TX/RX (Active Wardrive) or Auto RX (Passive Wardrive). 7. Walk/drive around and watch the tracking live. iOS 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App. 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore App. 3. Install Bluefy (Web BLE browser): https://apps.apple.com/us/app/bluefy-web-ble-browser/id1492822055 4. Open https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ inside Bluefy. 5. Connect your device to the page. 6. Choose your scan interval and whether you're using an external antenna or not. 7. Send a ping, or enable Auto TX/RX (Active Wardrive) or Auto RX (Passive Wardrive). 8. Move around and watch the tracking live. Native App Beta iOS: Join the TestFlight here: https://testflight.apple.com/join/nd3Rrkrb Android: DM me your email address

messages.reply_html:
Altitude YYC
I was messing around with MeshCore hashtag channels. I learned something new. As hashtag channels are like public channels; once you join the channel you are always "following" the channel. So you will receive new message notification as long as your node is turned on and connected to your mesh. If you leave the local mesh or turn off your node, you will not see any of the messages that were sent to the channel while you were offline. On the other hand, 'Room' servers will store messages (up to 32) and once you sign into the 'ROOM' and leave you node turned on you will receive notification of new messages. If you turn off your node or leave the local mesh you will have to log back into the 'Room' again to receive new messages. Seems like a hashtag channel linked with a Room Server would be a good combination for group or community chat. (edited)
